<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123530063]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = {{religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272 |location = |deity = [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]]、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the sacred territory of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]], 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]], [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]], [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]])<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Hashiba Hideyoshi]], the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April [[:en:1873|1873]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]]社, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]]社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272
== Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123530063]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = {{religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272 |location = |deity = [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]]、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the sacred territory of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]], 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]], [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]], [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]])<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Hashiba Hideyoshi]], the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April [[:en:1873|1873]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]]社, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]]社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272
== Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Hashiba Hideyoshi]], the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April [[:en:1873|1873]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]]社, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]]社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272
== Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]

'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the sacred territory of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]], 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]], [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]], [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]])<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Hashiba Hideyoshi]], the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April [[:en:1873|1873]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]]社, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]]社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q123530063]] Wikidata name: Watatsumi Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |image = {{religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272 |location = |deity = [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]]、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shrine]] located in the sacred territory of [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – [[:en:Watatsumi|Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto]], 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]], [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]], [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in [[:en:Kumano region|Kumano]])<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Hashiba Hideyoshi]], the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April [[:en:1873|1873]] (the 6th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September [[:en:1942|1942]] (the 17th year of [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]]), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, [[:en:Tamayori-hime|Tamayoribime Shrine]]社, [[:en:Toyotama-hime|Toyotama-hime Shrine]]社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, [[:en:Kotoshironushi|Kotoshiranushi]]命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kinokawa, Wakayama|Kinokawa City]]神領272
== Access == * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR]] [[:en:Wakayama Line|Wakayama Line]] [[:en:Uchita Station|Uchida Station]], it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}}
== External Links == * [https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]

'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.

== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.
== References ==

'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]]
{{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = {{religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272 |location = |deity = {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

{{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |image = {{religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272 |location = |deity = {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi}}命

In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.

In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.

== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Kinokawa City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]]
{{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = {{religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}神領272 |location = |deity = {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine|1=Tamayori-hime}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine|1=Tamayori-hime}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine|1=Toyotama-hime}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station|1=Uchita Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Q11604917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station|1=Uchita Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]] [[ja:海神社 (紀の川市)]]
{{infobox religious building |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = {{religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image=Image Needed|cat=Kinokawa City}} |name = Kaijin Shrine (Kinokawa) |native_name = 海神社 |a11ress = {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}神領272 |location = |deity = {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}、国津姫命 |神体 = |社格 = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = In the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} |本殿 = |別名 = Kaijin-san |札所等 = |festival = October 15 (Third Sunday of October) |神事 = |location map = }}
'''Kaijin Shrine''' (海神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the sacred territory of {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}. Its former Rank was {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. It is colloquially known as “Kaijin-san”.
== Deity == * Primary Deity – {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|WD=Q1474187|lt=Toyotama-hiko no Mikoto|1=Watatsumi}}, 国津姫命 * Auxiliary Deities – 穂高見命, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine|1=Toyotama-hime}}, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine|1=Tamayori-hime}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}命
== History == Although the exact date of its foundation is unknown, the shrine is said to have been established in the era of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} when {{ill|Imibe no Sukune|ja|忌部宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imibe no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received a divine revelation from the deity (the revered spirit that is said to have appeared at {{ill|Tategasaka|ja|楯ヶ崎|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tategasaka|1=Tategasaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541393}} in {{ill|Kumano region|en|Kumano region|WD=Q6443450|lt=Kumano|1=Kumano region}})<ref name="紀の川市 海神社">[https://www.city.kinokawa.lg.jp/kanko/temples_shrines/kaijinja.html Kinokawa City Official Website – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> which led to its establishment<ref name="海神社">[https://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=3022 Wakayama Prefecture Shrine Office – Kaijin Shrine]</ref>.
Since ancient times, the Imperial Court’s veneration was profound, and it appears to have possessed many sacred estates. The area in which the shrine is enshrined is called “Shinryō”, and as its name indicates, it was the shrine’s own territory<ref name="海神社"/>.
In the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13th year (1585), during the {{ill|Kishu Conquest|ja|紀州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishu Conquest|1=Battle of Kishuu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855721}} conducted by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, the main hall and two subsidiary shrines, the worship hall, the Kagura hall, the treasure repository, and other structures were completely destroyed by military fire, with treasures and ancient records also burned<ref name="海神社"/>.
In April {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (the 6th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In September {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}} (the 17th year of {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}}), it was promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}<ref name="海神社"/>.
To the north of the shrine is {{ill|Kaijin Pond|ja|海神池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaijin Pond|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Shrine Grounds == * Main Hall<ref name="海神社"/> * Long Platform – Ritual Hall * Worship Hall (Wari-haiden) * Sacred Food Preparation Area * Shrine Implements Repository * Shrine Office * Mikoshi Storehouse
=== Subordinate Shrines === * Eastern Subordinate Shrine – 穂高見命社, {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|WD=Q56344982|lt=Tamayoribime Shrine|1=Tamayori-hime}}社, {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|WD=Q2329261|WD=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime Shrine|1=Toyotama-hime}}社<ref name="kaijinnjya">[https://jun-yu-roku.com/kii-naga-jinryo-kai/ Shrine Tour – Kaijin Shrine]</ref> * Western Subordinate Shrine – 幸神社, {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshiranushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}命 * {{ill|Kōtaigu Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtaigu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * {{ill|Gion Shrine|ja|祇園社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祇園神社|1=Gion shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588442}}
== Address == * {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kinokawa, Wakayama|en|Kinokawa, Wakayama|WD=Q697002|lt=Kinokawa City|1=Kinokawa, Wakayama}}神領272
== Access == * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station|1=Uchita Station}}, take the {{ill|Kinokawa City Community Bus|ja|紀の川市地域巡回バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinokawa City Community Bus|1=Q11604917|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604917}} Northern Seida Course “Shinryō-kita” bus stop and walk for about 2 minutes. * From {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Wakayama Line|en|Wakayama Line|WD=Q511543|lt=Wakayama Line|1=Wakayama Line}} {{ill|Uchita Station|en|Uchita Station|WD=Q3193482|lt=Uchida Station|1=Uchita Station}}, it is approximately a 54‐minute walk.
